description_long: This is a two-story residential house with blue horizontal siding and a dark gray gable roof. The structure features a prominent front porch supported by four white columns and accessed by a small set of steps. Windows are arranged in a mix of symmetrical and asymmetrical patterns, with a distinctive octagonal window above the porch. The house shows signs of weathering, with visible patches and discoloration on the siding. The foundation appears to be concrete, and the trim is painted white, providing contrast to the blue exterior. The overall design is reminiscent of American Craftsman or Colonial Revival styles commonly found in suburban neighborhoods.
description: A blue two-story house with a dark gable roof, front porch with columns, and a mix of rectangular and octagonal windows. Weathered siding and white trim add character to its suburban appearance.
description_view_0: This view shows the side and partial front of the house, highlighting the gable roof, two rectangular windows, and the side of the front porch.
description_view_1: The front view displays the main entryway with a covered porch, four columns, steps, and the distinctive octagonal window above the porch.
description_view_2: This side view reveals a long wall with two rectangular windows and the expanse of the main roof, with the porch roof visible to the left.
description_view_3: The rear view shows a simple facade with two windows and visible weathering on the siding, as well as the back of the attached side structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with a mix of rectangular and octagonal windows
attribute_entryway_design: Front porch with four columns, small steps, overhanging roof, and a central door beneath an octagonal window
